title: Delhi EV policy: ₹1 lakh incentive for four-wheeler owners who switch to EVs, scrappage incentives explained
summary: Delhi's new electronic vehicles policy provides ₹1 lakh scrapping incentive for four-wheeler owners who switch to EVs from petrol and diesel vehicles. We explain how the incentives work and steps to apply…

title: Top 5 diversified equity funds based on 10-year returns: Nippon India Small Cap Fund leads with over 21% return
summary: Based on 10-year returns, Nippon India Small Cap Fund emerged as the best-performing diversified equity scheme, delivering a CAGR of 21.81% over the past decade. The rankings also highlight that Quant Mutual Fund had three schemes among the top five performers.

title: Nifty PSU Bank ETFs: Top 5 funds by AUM and their historical performance compared
summary: Among ETFs tracking the Nifty PSU Bank Index, Nippon India ETF Nifty PSU Bank BeES is the biggest fund with assets under management (AUM) of ₹4,078 crore. It is followed by Kotak Nifty PSU Bank ETF, while DSP Nifty PSU Bank ETF is the third biggest fund in the category.

title: Fallen trees, flooded roads, dead batteries: How motor OD cover protects you
summary: Own Damage cover protects vehicles from storms and floods, but payouts shrink sharply without add-ons like zero depreciation. A Mumbai case shows how skipping the fine print can cost thousands in unexpected out-of-pocket repairs.

title: Aggressive hybrid funds: 5 schemes gave 15%+ SIP returns in 10 years; check here
summary: The top aggressive hybrid funds generated annualised SIP returns of up to 18.57% over the past decade. Here's how the category's best-performing schemes fared across three-, five- and 10-year investment horizons.

title: 8th pay commission: DR and fitment factor hike, age-based enhancement — Here's a look at key pension revision demands
summary: Today we explore some of the key pension revision demands made by groups and what these could mean for retirees if accepted.

title: Dearness Allowance hike: From DA merger to inflation adjustment — Here are top 5 demands from employee groups
summary: Today we take a look at the top 5 DA related demands made by central govt employee representative groups, unions and stakeholders who collectively represent a large number of staff and pensioners, including defence and railway personnel.

title: Mutual fund NAV cut-off timings explained: Why investing or redeeming today doesn't always mean today's NAV
summary: For equity, hybrid, and most debt funds, the cut-off timing is 3:00 PM, while liquid and overnight funds follow different timelines, with a 1:30 PM purchase cut-off and a 3:00 PM redemption cut-off (extended to 7:00 PM for applications received through online mode).&nbsp;

title: ITR filing 2026: Have a US 401(k) or foreign pension? You may have to file ITR-2
summary: ITR rules for AY 2026-27 make taxpayers with foreign assets, overseas retirement accounts or foreign income ineligible for ITR-1 in many cases. Here's who may need to file ITR-2 instead, the reporting requirements, and what returning NRIs should know before filing their income tax return.

title: Ahead of Market: 10 things that will decide stock market action on Monday
summary: Indian stock markets surged on Friday, with Sensex and Nifty posting gains exceeding 0.3%. This upward trend was fueled by a calmer Middle East situation, reduced expectations of US Federal Reserve rate hikes, and positive domestic factors like the India-Japan Summit and IT sector recovery.

title: What Warren Buffett learnt from buying his first stock at age of 11 amid World War II
summary: Warren Buffett, commonly referred to as the ‘Oracle of Omaha', was born on August 30, 1930 in the town of Omaha, in the US. He studied under the legendary value investor Benjamin Graham while pursuing a business degree at Columbia University.

title: Buy Lodha Developers, Aditya Birla Real Estate; organised players to benefit from housing market consolidation: Siddhartha Khemka
summary: India's real estate market is seeing a K-shaped recovery, with major developers outperforming smaller ones. Leading companies are gaining market share due to strong finances and execution, while new project launches are consolidating among well-capitalised players.

title: 11 largecap stocks with upside potential of up to 50%. Do you own any?

Companies like Swiggy, ICICI Prudential Life, and HDFC Life Insurance are among those with strong 'Buy' ratings and substantial projected upside.

title: Bonus issues, stock splits & dividends: Titan, JSW Steel among 49 stocks turning ex-date this week. Do you own any?
summary: Investors must hold shares of these companies in their demat accounts on the record date to be eligible for the respective corporate actions. The list remains tentative, as more companies may announce record dates for dividends, bonus issues and stock splits during the week.

title: Vedanta, TCS among 5 stocks with the highest dividend yield. Check details
summary: Investors seeking high returns are eyeing stocks with impressive dividend yields. Vedanta leads the pack with a remarkable 12% yield, followed by Gujarat Pipavav Port at 6.7% and ONGC at 5.5%.

title: Will Sensex, Nifty continue to rally on Monday? Q1 earnings among 6 factors to drive Dalal Street this week
summary: Sensex gained around 262 points to close at 77,764 on Friday, while Nifty 50 rose more than 95 points to end the session above 24,270 during Friday's trading session. The sharp gains added nearly Rs 44,155 crore to the total market capitalisation of all companies listed on BSE, pulling it up to Rs 4
